summary.detailed_summary: The paper proposes Stacked LoRA, an adaptation framework designed to mitigate inter-subject variability when fine-tuning frozen EEG foundation models (REVE, LaBraM, and LUNA). The method structurally separates the low-rank update into a shared Global adapter for subject-invariant task knowledge and individual Subject-Specific adapters to absorb personal neural signatures. Experiments on three motor imagery datasets demonstrate that this decoupled approach generally outperforms single-path adaptation, with the optimal balance shifting toward global paths for large cohorts and subject-specific paths for high-variability clinical data.

headline_results: Stacked LoRA achieved best or tied-best balanced accuracy in 7 out of 9 backbone-dataset combinations.

key_points: Method for EEG-FM adaptation: Introduces Stacked LoRA to decouple subject-invariant and subject-specific knowledge during fine-tuning.
Versatile performance: Outperforms linear probing and single-path LoRA across REVE, LaBraM, and LUNA backbones on multiple MI datasets.
Population-dependent utility: Demonstrates that shared adapters suffice for large, diverse cohorts, while subject-specific paths are critical for high-variability clinical recordings.
limitations: Requires subject identity at inference time, preventing zero-shot personalization for unseen subjects.
Training cost increases linearly with the number of subjects due to joint optimization of all subject-specific adapters.
Subject-specific adapters offer diminishing returns when data per subject is very scarce.
Evaluation was restricted to motor imagery decoding with within-dataset splits.

method.architecture: Frozen backbone (REVE, LaBraM, LUNA) + Stacked LoRA (Global adapter r=32, Subject-Specific adapters r=8) + linear classification head.
method.finetuning: Joint optimization of Global and Subject-Specific LoRA weights using AdamW with cosine-annealing.
method.objective: Cross-entropy loss for motor imagery classification.
method.pretraining: Utilizes existing pretrained weights from REVE, LaBraM, and LUNA.

summary.one_liner: A dual-path LoRA framework that decouples global task knowledge from subject-specific signatures to improve the adaptation of EEG foundation models for motor imagery decoding.
summary.title: Stacked LoRA for Subject-Adaptive EEG Foundation Models in Motor Imagery Decoding
summary.unique_contribution: Proposes a dual-path adaptation architecture (Stacked LoRA) that explicitly decouples population-level task knowledge from individual subject variability during the fine-tuning of frozen EEG foundation models.

papers.title: Stacked LoRA for Subject-Adaptive EEG Foundation Models in Motor Imagery Decoding

reasons: Explicitly targets the adaptation and fine-tuning of existing EEG foundation models such as REVE, LaBraM, and LUNA.
Proposes a specific framework (Stacked LoRA) to decouple subject-invariant and subject-specific knowledge within EEG-FMs.
Focuses on the EEG-FM ecosystem by addressing the challenge of adapting large pretrained EEG models to downstream tasks.

papers.title: NeuroOnline: Bridging Pretraining and Online Adaptation for EEG Foundation Models

reasons: Explicitly targets EEG foundation models in both the title and abstract.
Proposes a framework for online adaptation and alignment of pretrained EEG representations.
Addresses the gap between pretraining and downstream deployment for EEG-FMs.
